title: Latest News from Science Magazine
description: Keyword search result for Alerts: News Weekly Headlines -- New results matching your keyword search query (updated daily).

items.title: Analysis: Why the research money isn’t flowing from NSF and NIH
items.description: White House review of agency spending plans for this year is causing delays

items.title: India’s national AI platform tackles the country’s many tongues
items.description: Bhashini aims to put the power of AI in the hands of all Indians

items.title: A Brazilian scientist’s experimental paralysis therapy is fueling hope—and hype
items.description: Spinal cord injury patients are scrambling for access to the injection, but researchers say preliminary evidence is weak

items.title: A forgotten social media post may hold key clues to COVID-19’s origin
items.description: Analysis of Wuhan market map suggests China has not disclosed some of the earliest infections in animals and people

items.title: Bronze Age loom weaves a tale of ancient textiles
items.description: A collapsed roof preserved wood from one of the world’s oldest weaving machines

items.title: Earth’s first major extinction was worse than we thought
items.description: Fossil finds suggest nearly 80% of life on Earth died some 550 million years ago

items.title: United States is cutting ties with influential global cancer agency
items.description: Trump’s pullout from WHO bars federal scientists from working with the International Agency for Research on Cancer and could slash its funding

items.title: NASA kills future x-ray mission
items.description: $1 billion AXIS proposal fell victim to agency upheaval, principal investigator says

items.title: India scraps new earthquake hazard map and building codes, drawing pushback from geologists
items.description: Concerns about cost and practicality sparked backlash from developers and engineers

items.title: Relic of long-vanished ice sheet holds clues to ancient climate
items.description: Glacial ice melting out of Alaska’s eroding coastline offers a glimpse into a lost climate history
